steps.title: Recognize what p(0) represents
steps.content: $p(0)$ is the output of the polynomial when the input is $x = 0$. Graphically, this is the $y$-intercept—the point where the curve crosses the $y$-axis.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the given points
steps.content: We are given that the graph passes through four points. One of them has $x$-coordinate equal to 0: the point $(0, 7)$. At $x = 0$, the $y$-value is 7, so $p(0) = 7$.
